title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But the {{c1::economic impact seems}}(经济影响似乎) to be {{c2::dramatically behind}}(远远落后). It’s very difficult to {{c3::make sense of}}(理解), how can the model, {{c4::on the one hand}}(一方面), do these amazing things, and then {{c5::on the other hand}}(另一方面), repeat itself twice in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 但经济影响似乎远远落后。很难理解，模型怎么会在一方面做出这些惊人的事情，另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次？
note:
phrases: economic impact seems | dramatically behind | make sense of | on the one hand | on the other hand
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0004_b5466e
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: discourse_organizer
transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.

text: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to {{c1::make sense of}}(理解), how can the model, {{c2::on the one hand}}(一方面), do these amazing things, and then {{c3::on the other hand}}(另一方面), {{c4::repeat itself twice}}(重复两次) in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There’ll be very strong {{c1::economic forces for}}(经济力量) this, and I think the {{c2::impact is going}}(影响正在) to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you {{c3::expect that impact}}(预计会产生影响)?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
translation: 这背后会有非常强大的经济力量推动，我认为其影响将会非常显著。Dwarkesh Patel 您预计这种影响会何时出现？
note:
phrases: economic forces for | impact is going | expect that impact
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: The idea that {{c1::we’d be investing}}(我们会投入) 1% of GDP in AI, {{c2::I feel like}}(我觉得) it would have felt like {{c3::a bigger deal}}(更大的事), whereas {{c4::right now}}(现在) it just feels... Ilya Sutskever
original: The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels... Ilya Sutskever
translation: Ilya Sutskever 我认为，如果我们将1%的GDP投资于AI，这本应是一件大事，但现在却只是觉得...
note:
phrases: we’d be investing | I feel like | a bigger deal | right now
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you {{c1::tell the model}}(告诉模型), “Can you {{c2::please fix the bug}}(请修复这个错误)?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me {{c3::go fix that}}(去修复那个).”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“能帮我修复这个 bug 吗？”模型说：“天啊，您说得对。我确实有 bug。让我去修一下。”
note:
phrases: tell the model | please fix the bug | go fix that
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: This is one of the {{c1::very confusing things}}(非常令人困惑的事情) {{c2::about the models}}(关于这些模型) right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are {{c3::doing so well}}(表现这么好) on evals?
original: This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
translation: 这正是目前模型中最令人困惑的地方之一。如何调和它们在评估测试中表现如此出色这一事实？
note:
phrases: very confusing things | about the models | doing so well

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell it, “You {{c1::have this new}}(拥有这个全新的) second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re {{c2::so right again}}(又对了),”
original: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,”
translation: 然后你告诉它：“你又出现了新的第二个 bug”，它告诉你：“天啊，我怎么会犯这种错？您说得对极了。”

phrases: have this new | so right again

title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever Sure. Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how {{c1::from the average person}}(普通人)’s point of view {{c2::nothing is that different}}(并没有什么不同), {{c3::will continue being true}}(将继续保持真实) even into the singularity.
original: Ilya Sutskever Sure. Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view nothing is that different, will continue being true even into the singularity.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 当然。德瓦雷什·帕特尔 我认为你的观点，即从普通人角度来看，事物并没有什么不同，即使在奇点到来之后，也会继续保持真实。
note:
phrases: from the average person | nothing is that different | will continue being true

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend {{c1::dollar amount of investment}}(投资的金额). I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
original: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 我提到的那种不觉得不同的感觉是，比如某某公司宣布了一笔难以理解的巨额投资。我认为没有人知道该如何利用这些资金。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of {{c1::what data to train}}(要训练什么数据) on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
original: Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
translation: 在进行预训练的时候，训练数据的问题已经有了答案，因为答案就是所有数据。当你进行预训练时，你需要所有的数据。
note:
phrases: what data to train

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: An example would be, {{c1::let’s say}}(假设) you {{c2::use vibe coding}}(使用VIBE编码) to do something. You {{c3::go to some place}}(去某个地方) and then you {{c4::get a bug}}(发现一个 bug).

translation: 比如，假设你使用了振动编码来做某件事。你去了某个地方，然后遇到了一个bug。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(两种可能的解释). The more {{c2::whimsical explanation}}(奇特的解释) is that maybe {{c3::RL training makes}}(强化学习训练能) the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
translation: 我有两个可能的解释。更有趣的解释是，也许强化学习训练让模型变得过于专注，过于狭隘，在某种程度上让它们变得不那么敏感，尽管它也让它们在其他方面变得更加敏感。
note:
phrases: two possible explanations | whimsical explanation | RL training makes
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce {{c1::new RL environments}}(新的强化学习环境) and just add it to the {{c2::training mix}}(训练混合). The question is, well, what are those?
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those?
translation: 据我了解，所有公司都有团队专门开发新的强化学习环境，并将其添加到训练混合中。问题是，那些是什么？

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But when people do {{c1::RL training}}(强化学习训练), they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this {{c2::kind of RL}}(一种强化学习) training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
original: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
translation: 不过，当人们进行强化学习训练时，确实需要动脑筋。他们会想：“好的，我们需要这种类型的强化学习训练来解决这个问题，而那种类型的强化学习训练用于解决那个问题。”
note:
phrases: RL training | kind of RL

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Because of this, they {{c1::can’t do basic things}}(无法完成基本任务). But {{c2::there is another explanation}}(还有另一种解释).
original: Because of this, they can’t do basic things. But there is another explanation.
translation: 因此，他们无法完成一些基本的事情。不过，还有另一种解释。
